proposed_hypotheses.text: pfs_months varies meaningfully across patients (mean ~4 months, SD ~2, distribution non-degenerate), indicating that variation in features should be predictive.

analyses.result_summary: pfs_months has mean 4.31, SD 2.29, median 4.21, range 0.00-16.25 across N=50000. Distribution is right-skewed but non-degenerate.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive slope) in this colorectal cohort.
Female sex (sex_female=1) is associated with different pfs_months than male sex.

analyses.result_summary: Each additional year of age_years is associated with +0.177 months of pfs_months (univariate OLS, p<1e-300, R^2=0.59). Strongly supports the hypothesis.
Mean pfs_months differs by only -0.006 months between female and male patients (univariate OLS p=0.77). No detectable effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
stage_iv=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0.
right_sided_primary=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than right_sided_primary=0.

analyses.result_summary: Each unit increase in ecog_ps is associated with -1.190 months pfs (univariate, p<1e-300). Strong negative effect.
stage_iv=1 patients have pfs that is 1.352 months shorter than stage_iv=0 (p<1e-300). Strong negative effect.
Right-sided primary tumors have 0.305 months shorter pfs (p=5e-46). Effect is real but smaller than ECOG/stage.

proposed_hypotheses.text: kras_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than kras_mutation=0.
nras_mutation=1 is associated with different pfs_months than nras_mutation=0.
braf_v600e=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than braf_v600e=0.
msi_high=1 is associated with different pfs_months than msi_high=0.
her2_amplified=1 is associated with different pfs_months than her2_amplified=0.
ntrk_fusion=1 is associated with different pfs_months than ntrk_fusion=0.

analyses.result_summary: kras_mutation=1 has pfs 0.327 months shorter (p=8.5e-56). Apparent negative prognostic effect (later we discover this is largely confounded with treatment heterogeneity).
nras_mutation=1 patients have pfs +0.221 months longer (p=2e-4). Small but detectable positive direction.
braf_v600e=1 patients have pfs 0.228 months shorter (p=4e-6). Negative association.
msi_high=1 vs 0 difference is -0.019 months (p=0.68). No detectable main effect.
her2_amplified main effect is -0.056 months (p=0.35). No detectable main effect.
ntrk_fusion main effect is +0.04 months (p=0.78). No detectable main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive slope).
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Higher nlr is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).

analyses.result_summary: Each g/dL increase in albumin_g_dl is associated with +0.465 months pfs (p=3e-115). Strongly supports the hypothesis.
Each 1% weight loss in 6 mo is associated with -0.073 months pfs (p=2e-167). Strongly supports.
Each ng/mL of cea_ng_ml is associated with -0.0054 months pfs (p=3e-17). Supports the hypothesis.
Each U/L of ldh_u_l is associated with -0.00032 months pfs (p=0.013). Weak but supportive.
crp_mg_l univariate slope is -0.002 (p=0.065). Borderline; not formally significant.
nlr univariate slope +0.005 (p=0.29). Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_cetuximab=1 patients have different pfs_months than treatment_cetuximab=0 (crude).
treatment_bevacizumab=1 patients have different pfs_months than treatment_bevacizumab=0 (crude).
treatment_pembrolizumab=1 patients have different pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab=0 (crude).
treatment_encorafenib=1 patients have different pfs_months than treatment_encorafenib=0 (crude).
treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=1 patients have different pfs_months than treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=0 (crude).
treatment_regorafenib=1 patients have longer pfs_months than treatment_regorafenib=0 (crude).

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs 4.285 (n=15078) on cetuximab vs 4.323 (n=34922) off; difference -0.038 (p=0.089). Not significant.
Mean pfs 4.301 on bevacizumab vs 4.320 off; difference -0.019 (p=0.35). Not significant.
Mean pfs 4.316 on pembrolizumab vs 4.311 off; difference +0.005 (p=0.86). Not significant.
Mean pfs 4.316 on encorafenib vs 4.311 off; difference +0.005 (p=0.87). Not significant.
Mean pfs 4.271 on trastuzumab/tucatinib vs 4.315 off; difference -0.044 (p=0.25). Not significant.
Mean pfs 5.089 on regorafenib (n=10022) vs 4.117 off (n=39978); difference +0.972 months (p<1e-300). Strongly supports a positive crude main effect for regorafenib.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment for age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, biomarkers, and labs, treatment_regorafenib retains a positive main effect on pfs_months while the other five treatments do not.
After adjustment, kras_mutation, braf_v600e, and right_sided_primary are independently associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: In a 33-covariate OLS, treatment_regorafenib coefficient = +0.943 months (p<1e-300). Cetuximab -0.011 (p=0.19), bevacizumab +0.003 (p=0.73), pembrolizumab +0.0003 (p=0.98), encorafenib +0.006 (p=0.63), trastuzumab/tucatinib +0.026 (p=0.07). Only regorafenib has a real adjusted main effect.

Adjusted: kras_mutation -0.354 (p<1e-43), braf_v600e -0.357 (p<1e-78), right_sided_primary -0.287 (p<1e-200). All three remain significant negative associations after adjustment. msi_high (p=0.66), her2_amplified (p=0.48), ntrk_fusion (p=0.47), nras_mutation (p=0.37) had no adjusted main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_cetuximab effect on pfs_months is larger (less negative or more positive) in kras_mutation=0 than kras_mutation=1 (clinical prior: anti-EGFR works in RAS WT).
treatment_cetuximab effect on pfs_months is larger in nras_mutation=0 than nras_mutation=1.
treatment_cetuximab effect on pfs_months is larger in braf_v600e=0 than braf_v600e=1.
treatment_cetuximab effect on pfs_months is larger in left-sided (right_sided_primary=0) than right-sided primaries.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction term cetuximab*kras = +0.022 (p=0.25). Effect in KRAS WT = -0.020 mo, effect in KRAS mutant = +0.002 mo. No statistically significant heterogeneity, contrary to clinical prior. Hypothesis not supported.
Interaction = -0.079 (p=0.16). Effect in NRAS WT = -0.007, NRAS mutant = -0.086. Trend is in the predicted direction (worse in NRAS mutant) but not significant.
Interaction = +0.002 (p=0.96). No interaction.
Interaction = +0.022 (p=0.25). No statistically significant heterogeneity. Hypothesis not supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab increases pfs_months in msi_high=1 patients but not msi_high=0 patients (clinical prior: ICI works in MMR-deficient/MSI-H CRC).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction pembro*msi_high = +0.056 (p=0.30). Effect in MSI-H = +0.055 mo (n_treated=396), effect in MSS = -0.002 mo. Direction is consistent with prior but the magnitude is tiny and not significant. Crude check: mean pfs in MSI-H on pembro 4.30 vs off 4.29 (delta +0.007). Hypothesis not supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_encorafenib increases pfs_months in braf_v600e=1 patients but not braf_v600e=0 patients (clinical prior).
Combined treatment_encorafenib + treatment_cetuximab among braf_v600e=1 patients is associated with longer pfs_months than neither among braf_v600e=1 (BEACON regimen prior).

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction = +0.004 (p=0.95). Crude eff in BRAF V600E = -0.13 mo (NS); in BRAF WT = +0.01. No benefit of encorafenib in V600E patients. Hypothesis not supported.

analyses.code: Among braf_v600e=1: mean pfs by enco+cet combination
analyses.result_summary: BRAF V600E patients on both encorafenib AND cetuximab (n=58): mean pfs 4.151 vs all others among V600E (n=2214): 4.093 (delta +0.058). Tiny effect, not statistically significant. Hypothesis not supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib increases pfs_months in her2_amplified=1 patients but not her2_amplified=0 patients (clinical prior: HER2 targeting in HER2+ CRC).

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction = -0.007 (p=0.94). Effect in HER2+ (n_treated=120): +0.011 mo; in HER2- : +0.018 mo. Mean pfs HER2+ on tx 4.273 vs off 4.256 (delta +0.017). No benefit. Hypothesis not supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_bevacizumab effect on pfs_months differs by right_sided_primary status.
treatment_bevacizumab effect on pfs_months is larger in msi_high=1 (data-driven from screening).

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction = -0.0001 (p=0.99). No interaction; bevacizumab effect in left-sided +0.003, in right-sided +0.003. Hypothesis not supported.
Interaction = +0.085 (p=0.033). Marginal but isolated; given multiple-testing burden across the screen, treated as a chance finding rather than signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months is larger in kras_mutation=0 than kras_mutation=1 (data-driven hypothesis after observing crude regorafenib main effect).

analyses.code: OLS interaction regorafenib*kras_mutation, plus crude subgroup means
analyses.result_summary: Crude regorafenib effect in KRAS WT (n_on=5816) = +1.656 mo, in KRAS mutant (n_on=4206) = +0.027 mo. Adjusted interaction term = -1.626 (p<1e-300). KRAS mutation appears to fully ablate the regorafenib benefit. Hypothesis strongly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months is larger in braf_v600e=0 than braf_v600e=1.
treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months is larger in right_sided_primary=0 (left-sided) than right_sided_primary=1.

analyses.result_summary: Crude regorafenib effect in BRAF WT (n_on=9554) = +1.027 mo, in BRAF V600E (n_on=468) = -0.156 mo. Adjusted interaction = -0.992 (p=5e-98). BRAF V600E ablates the regorafenib benefit. Strongly supported.

Crude regorafenib effect in left-sided (n_on=6524) = +1.480 mo, in right-sided (n_on=3498) = +0.026 mo. Adjusted interaction = -1.456 (p<1e-300). Right-sided primary ablates the benefit. Strongly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months does NOT differ by nras_mutation, msi_high, her2_amplified, ntrk_fusion, or sex_female (after observing strong KRAS/BRAF/sidedness effects, test other modifiers to localize the responder definition).

analyses.code: OLS interactions regorafenib*{nras_mutation, msi_high, her2_amplified, ntrk_fusion, sex_female}
analyses.result_summary: regorafenib*nras_mutation interaction = +0.893 (p=2e-54). Effect in NRAS mutant (n_on=313) = +1.808 mo, in NRAS WT (n_on=9709) = +0.915 mo. Surprising: NRAS mutation does NOT suppress and may actually enhance the regorafenib effect (because most NRAS-mutant patients in this cohort happen to be KRAS WT/BRAF WT/left-sided). regorafenib*msi_high = -0.072 (p=0.12), regorafenib*her2_amplified = -0.065 (p=0.26), regorafenib*ntrk = +0.23 (p=0.12), regorafenib*sex = +0.045 (p=0.026, marginal). Net: NRAS, MSI-H, HER2, NTRK, sex are not credible modifiers; the suppressors are KRAS, BRAF, and right-sidedness.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 patients, treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months more than in patients with at least one of kras_mutation=1 or braf_v600e=1.

analyses.code: Subgroup OLS: regorafenib effect in KRAS WT & BRAF WT vs elsewhere
analyses.result_summary: In KRAS WT & BRAF WT (n_on=5348, n_off=21482): regorafenib effect = +1.814 mo. Outside (n_on=4674, n_off=18496): effect = +0.008 mo. Refined two-way subgroup is supported, but right-sided patients within the KRAS/BRAF WT group still dilute the effect, so further refinement is needed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 (left-sided RAS/RAF wild-type) patients, treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months substantially more than outside this three-way subgroup.

analyses.code: Subgroup OLS: regorafenib effect in KRAS WT & BRAF WT & left-sided
analyses.result_summary: In KRAS WT & BRAF WT & left-sided (n_on=3461, n_off=13911): mean pfs on regorafenib 6.88 vs off 4.12; effect = +2.758 mo. In the complement (n_on=6561, n_off=26067): effect = +0.030 mo. Adjusted three-way subgroup interaction term = +2.737 (p<1e-300, t=172). Strongly supported as a real subgroup definition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responder subgroup (kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0), continuous biomarkers (cea_ng_ml, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l, nlr, hemoglobin_g_dl, age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv) further modify the treatment_regorafenib effect.

analyses.result_summary: Within responder subgroup, regorafenib*cea_ng_ml interaction = -0.0649 per ng/mL (p<1e-100). Other modifiers tested: regorafenib*albumin = -0.153 (p=0.09), regorafenib*ldh = -0.00052 (p=0.33), regorafenib*weight_loss = +0.003 (p=0.79), regorafenib*crp = +0.0024 (p=0.63), regorafenib*nlr = +0.048 (p=0.028, weak), regorafenib*age = +0.003 (p=0.29), regorafenib*ecog = +0.053 (p=0.39), regorafenib*stage_iv = +0.240 (p=0.006). The dominant modifier within the subgroup is cea_ng_ml: each +1 ng/mL CEA reduces regorafenib benefit by ~0.065 months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responder subgroup, the treatment_regorafenib effect is concentrated in patients with cea_ng_ml below approximately 5 ng/mL (i.e., low CEA marks a sharp response cutoff).

analyses.code: CEA-binned regorafenib effect within KRAS WT & BRAF WT & left-sided
analyses.result_summary: Within the three-way responder subgroup, CEA-binned effects are: CEA [0,5) +5.013 (n_on=1903, n_off=7423); [5,10) +0.028; [10,20) +0.053; [20,50) -0.216; [50,100) +0.029; [100+) +0.967. The benefit collapses sharply once CEA exceeds 5 ng/mL — consistent with a CEA<5 ng/mL threshold. Outside the three-way subgroup, the effect is null at every CEA bin (range -0.02 to +0.25). The four-way subgroup (KRAS WT AND BRAF V600E negative AND left-sided AND CEA < 5 ng/mL) defines the regorafenib responders.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Defining responder_4way = 1 if (kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND cea_ng_ml < 5), the adjusted regorafenib*responder_4way interaction effect on pfs_months is on the order of +5 months, while the within-stratum main treatment effect is null.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted regorafenib*responder_4way coefficient = +4.981 mo (SE 0.006, t=893, p<1e-300). The unstratified treatment_regorafenib coefficient is -0.003 (p=0.28) and responder_4way main effect is -0.006 (p=0.08). Within responder_4way=1 (n=9326): mean pfs on regorafenib 9.02 vs off 4.01, effect +5.013 (t-test p<1e-300, t=92.65). Outside (n=40674): effect +0.025 (p=0.33). Strongly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the four-way regorafenib responder subgroup, the effect of treatment_regorafenib is not further modified by nras_mutation, msi_high, her2_amplified, ntrk_fusion, or sex_female (i.e., the four-way definition is sufficient).

analyses.code: Within KRAS WT & BRAF WT & left & CEA<5: stratified mean pfs on/off regorafenib by each modifier
analyses.result_summary: Within the responder subgroup (KRAS WT & BRAF WT & left & CEA<5, n=9326), regorafenib effect estimates by further modifier: NRAS WT +5.00 vs NRAS mut +5.24 (delta +0.24, n_on/mut=113); MSS +5.00 vs MSI-H +5.28 (delta +0.27, n_on/MSI-H=89); HER2- +5.02 vs HER2+ +4.69 (delta -0.33, n_on/HER2+=54); NTRK- +5.01 vs NTRK+ +4.90 (delta -0.11, n_on/NTRK+=11); male +4.97 vs female +5.07 (delta +0.09). All deltas are within sampling noise and none change the qualitative four-way subgroup definition. Supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The kras_mutation=-0.35, braf_v600e=-0.36, and right_sided_primary=-0.29 'prognostic' main effects observed earlier are largely or fully explained by the suppression of the regorafenib effect within those strata; once the responder_4way x regorafenib interaction is in the model, those covariate main effects vanish.

analyses.code: OLS(pfs ~ covars + biomarkers + treatments + responder_4way + treatment_regorafenib:responder_4way)
analyses.result_summary: After adding the regorafenib*responder_4way interaction and main responder_4way term to the multivariable model, the residual main effects of kras_mutation (-0.003, p=0.17), braf_v600e (+0.001, p=0.79), right_sided_primary (+0.0004, p=0.84), and cea_ng_ml (-6e-5, p=0.34) drop to ~0. Hypothesis supported: those covariates were not truly prognostic in the no-treatment baseline; their apparent negative association arose because they identify regorafenib non-responders.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_cetuximab x kras_mutation x right_sided_primary x braf_v600e three- or four-way interactions on pfs_months are negligible (i.e., cetuximab has no biomarker-defined responder subgroup, despite clinical priors).
treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months remains null even within msi_high=1 stratified by other features (no hidden responder subgroup).

analyses.code: Among KRAS WT & NRAS WT & BRAF WT & left-sided (n=8866): mean pfs on/off cetuximab
analyses.result_summary: Even in the most clinically appropriate cetuximab subgroup (RAS/RAF wild-type, left-sided), mean pfs on cetuximab 4.32 vs off 4.36 (delta -0.04, p=0.42). No detectable cetuximab responder subgroup. Hypothesis (no benefit) supported.

analyses.code: Among MSI-H, mean pfs on/off pembrolizumab
analyses.result_summary: Among msi_high=1 (n=2513): pembro on 4.299 vs off 4.292 (delta +0.007). No effect; even further stratification (e.g., MSI-H + left-sided + KRAS WT) remains null. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Of the six available treatments, only treatment_regorafenib produces a clinically and statistically detectable PFS benefit, and that benefit is concentrated in the four-way subgroup defined by kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND cea_ng_ml<5 (final consolidated treatment-effect heterogeneity hypothesis).

analyses.code: Final OLS with responder_4way interaction; sensitivity to CEA threshold
analyses.result_summary: Effect of regorafenib in responder_4way=1 (KRAS WT & BRAF WT & left-sided & CEA<5): +5.01 mo (n=9326; t=92.7, p<1e-300; adjusted interaction +4.98 mo). Outside this subgroup: +0.025 mo (NS). CEA threshold sensitivity: cutoffs 2, 3, and 5 ng/mL all yield within-subgroup effects of ~+5.0 mo; cutoff 10 ng/mL dilutes to +3.6 mo. Cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, and trastuzumab/tucatinib have null adjusted main effects and no convincing biomarker-defined subgroup of benefit anywhere in the cohort. Hypothesis strongly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The four-way regorafenib responder subgroup is robust to adjustment for the strong baseline prognostic factors age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, ldh_u_l, and to inclusion of indicators for the other five treatments (i.e., the +5-month interaction effect is not a confounded artifact).

Variables whose unfavorable values suppress the regorafenib effect are kras_mutation=1, braf_v600e=1, right_sided_primary=1, and cea_ng_ml >= 5 ng/mL; each of these alone is sufficient to ablate the benefit (no patient with any unfavorable value derives the +5-month benefit).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with all 27 covariates + 6 treatment indicators + responder_4way + regorafenib*responder_4way
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted regorafenib*responder_4way coefficient = +4.981 mo (95% CI 4.97 to 4.99; t=893; p<1e-300). Coefficient is essentially unchanged (4.97-4.99) when prognostic covariates are added or removed, confirming robustness. Other treatment main effects remain near zero in this model.

analyses.code: Stratified subgroup means: regorafenib effect when responder is broken by each unfavorable feature
analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib effect when only one unfavorable feature is present (otherwise responder-eligible): KRAS WT & BRAF WT & left & CEA>=5 (n_on=1558): +0.005 mo; KRAS mut alone (BRAF WT & left & CEA<5, n_on=1477): +0.073 mo; BRAF V600E alone (n_on=154): -0.175 mo; right-sided alone (n_on=1020): +0.112 mo. Each unfavorable feature is independently sufficient to suppress the +5-month benefit; the full effect requires all four favorable conditions simultaneously (KRAS WT AND BRAF V600E negative AND left-sided AND CEA<5). Hypothesis supported.
